authoritative_sources.title: Interagency Guidance on Third-Party Relationships: Risk Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, FDIC, and OCC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary banking-agency guidance for due diligence, contract, monitoring, and issue-escalation controls across bank-fintech third-party relationships.

authoritative_sources.title: Conducting Due Diligence on Financial Technology Companies: A Guide for Community Banks
authoritative_sources.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, FDIC, and OCC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Joint banking-agency operating guide describing the fintech relationship domains, documentation, systems, and dependency checks used in sponsor-bank-backed programs.

authoritative_sources.title: Joint Statement on Banks’ Arrangements with Third Parties to Deliver Bank Deposit Products and Services
authoritative_sources.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, FDIC, and OCC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary 2024 interagency statement on operational, compliance, complaint, error-resolution, deposit-insurance, and AML risks in third-party deposit arrangements commonly used by neobanks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Servicing controls, complaint handling, and third-party deposit risk review

authoritative_sources.title: Banking With Third-Party Apps
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FDIC consumer guidance explaining how bank-fintech app arrangements, app outages, and pass-through deposit-insurance conditions affect customer servicing and communication.

authoritative_sources.title: Electronic Fund Transfers FAQs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CFPB FAQ set covering Regulation E treatment of P2P transfers, prepaid and mobile accounts, and non-bank providers that may still have error-resolution obligations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: P2P, wallet, and non-bank provider error-resolution intake

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR 1005.11 Procedures for Resolving Errors
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative Regulation E rule text for investigation timing, provisional-credit handling, and customer notification in EFT error-resolution work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Dispute investigation, provisional-credit, and notification timing
used_for: blocked-state handling when records are missing

authoritative_sources.title: Spring 2024 FDIC Consumer Compliance Supervisory Highlights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FDIC supervisory publication documenting how outsourced EFT dispute investigations can fail when third parties confuse chargeback or network logic with Regulation E obligations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Outsourced dispute review and failure-mode detection

authoritative_sources.title: Questions and Answers Related to the FDIC’s Part 328 Final Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FDIC implementation Q&A for digital signs, non-deposit signs, and insured-status communications on websites, apps, ATMs, and other channels used by neobanks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Digital disclosure and insured-status evidence control

authoritative_sources.title: Pass-through Deposit Insurance Coverage
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FDIC guidance defining the fiduciary, ownership, and recordkeeping requirements that must be satisfied before pass-through deposit insurance can be represented safely.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: FBO, custodial, and beneficial-owner evidence validation

authoritative_sources.title: Suspicious Activity Report Supporting Documentation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FinCEN guidance defining what suspicious-activity supporting documentation must be retained and how it may be disclosed, constraining handoffs from neobank service into AML-sensitive workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Suspicious-activity evidence handling and disclosure boundary control

authoritative_sources.title: Risk Management Topics - Fraud Monitoring Phase 2
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized ACH operating-rules guidance defining risk-based fraud monitoring, false-pretense handling, annual review expectations, and role-specific controls for ACH participants and service providers.
used_for: adjacent-lane routing to fraud and AML owners

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and neobank program grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the sponsor bank, program id, product model, jurisdiction, account type, transaction rail, and case identifiers before responding.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Record-of-truth retrieval across sponsor bank and third parties
workflow_stages.description: Verify sponsor-bank core records, middleware ledger entries, processor events, complaint notes, and communication history before stating status or next steps.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Supported servicing resolution and safe communication
workflow_stages.description: Respond using documented servicing state, dispute timing, account restrictions, and next-owner boundaries without promising unsupported credits, releases, or finality.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Complaint, repeat-failure, and unsupported exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package repeat failures, unsupported workarounds, approval-bound requests, and unresolved complaint history for the correct owner with an auditable communication trail.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Deposit insurance and disclosure boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Confirm named-bank, FBO, and beneficial-owner evidence before communicating insured status, digital-sign posture, or pass-through coverage and refuse unsupported statements.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Fraud, AML, and ACH false-pretense boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when suspicious-activity signals, ACH false-pretenses, KYC issues, or account-risk controls materially change the answer and do not disclose SAR posture or investigation status.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: sponsor-bank, program, product-model, and jurisdiction records
account profile, FBO or custodial account descriptors, customer communication logs, and servicing notes
sponsor-bank core records, middleware ledger entries, memo-post balances, holds, and restriction markers
card, ACH, P2P, wallet, dispute, provisional-credit, and processor event artifacts
complaint records, escalation packets, callback logs, and approval-history artifacts
deposit-insurance disclosures, digital-sign assets, named-bank references, and pass-through ownership records
KYC, CIP, fraud-monitoring, AML, suspicious-activity, and privacy-sensitive evidence markers
systems_of_record: card processor or debit network case system
fraud monitoring, AML review, and KYC platforms

allowed: Resolve informational neobank servicing requests when the answer can be grounded in authoritative sponsor-bank, ledger, processor, and case records.
Request the minimum missing identifiers, program facts, and source records needed to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed communication summaries, resolution notes, and escalation packets without overstating authority.
Route the case to the correct adjacent owner when the workflow becomes fraud, AML, disputes operations, sponsor-bank approval, or legal work.
escalate: Required sponsor-bank, program, product-model, or source records are missing or contradictory.
The request seeks a policy exception, manual credit, hold release, account reopening, or restriction override outside documented authority.
Fraud, AML, suspicious-activity, or consumer-impact complaint signals appear.
Public guidance and tenant or sponsor-bank policy materially disagree on what may be communicated, credited, released, or disclosed.
refuse: Requests for legal interpretation, regulated advice, approval authority, or account-risk override.
Requests to state that a fintech or nonbank failure is FDIC-insured or that pass-through coverage applies without required records.
Requests to disclose SAR posture, investigative details, or unsupported transaction truth.
Requests to treat neobank servicing as generic app support that ignores sponsor-bank and processor evidence.

domain_failure_modes: treating sponsor-bank-backed neobank servicing as generic app support and omitting sponsor-bank, program, and product-model dependencies
asserting pass-through or FDIC coverage without named-bank, fiduciary, and beneficial-owner records
using CRM or app notes as final truth when sponsor-bank core, middleware ledger, and processor records conflict
allowing a third-party dispute processor to deny or close an EFT dispute without a documented Regulation E investigation
promising manual credits, funds release, account reopening, or complaint closure without documented sponsor-bank or risk-owner approval
disclosing or implying suspicious-activity or SAR posture during ordinary service communication
closing repeat complaints without a routed escalation package and communication trail
using the neobank label cosmetically while failing to encode real fintech servicing controls, disclosures, and authority limits

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because sponsor-bank, processor, and policy conflicts are explicit escalation conditions rather than guessed outcomes.
The lane is bounded to neobank servicing work and must not imply sponsor-bank, legal, fraud, AML, or approval authority.
